content:
# Acts Chapter 22 -- The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus, PDF pages 253-254 | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/ACT_022_page_001.jpg, ACT_022_page_002.jpg

---

**1** "Men, brothers and fathers, hear my defense [which is] now to you."

**2** And having-heard that in-the Hebrew dialect he-was-addressing them, they-showed more quietness. And he-says,

**3** "I am a man, a Jew, having-been-born in Tarsus of-Cilicia, but having-been-brought-up in this city, at the feet of Gamaliel, having-been-instructed according-to [the] exactness of-the ancestral law, being a zealot of-God just-as all you are today.

**4** Who persecuted this Way unto death, binding and delivering-over into prisons both men and women,

**5** as also the high-priest bears-witness to-me, and all the council-of-elders; from whom also having-received letters to the brothers, to Damascus I-was-journeying, to-bring also those there being, having-been-bound, to Jerusalem, that they-might-be-punished.

**6** And it-came-to-pass [to] me, journeying and drawing-near to Damascus, about midday, suddenly out-of the heaven a great light shone-around me,

**7** and I-fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to-me, 'Saul, Saul, why are-you-persecuting me?'

**8** And I answered, 'Who are-you, Lord?' And he-said to me, 'I am Jesus the Nazorean, whom you are-persecuting.'

**9** And those being with me the light indeed beheld, but the voice they-did-not-hear of-the-[one] speaking to-me.

**10** And I-said, 'What shall-I-do, Lord?' And the Lord said to me, 'Having-risen-up, go into Damascus, and there it-shall-be-told to-you concerning all-things which have-been-appointed for-you to-do.'

**11** And as I-was-not-seeing from the glory of-that light, being-led-by-the-hand by those being-with me, I-came into Damascus.

**12** And a certain Ananias, a devout man according-to the law, being-witnessed-to by all the Jews dwelling-[there],

**13** having-come to me and having-stood-beside-[me] said to-me, 'Brother Saul, receive-sight.' And I in-that-same hour looked-up at him.

**14** And he said, 'The God of-our fathers appointed you to-know his will and to-see the Righteous-One and to-hear a voice from his mouth,

**15** because you-shall-be a witness for-him to all men of-what you-have-seen and heard.

**16** And now, why are-you-delaying? Having-risen-up, be-baptized and wash-away your sins, having-called-upon his name.'

**17** And it-came-to-pass [to] me, having-returned to Jerusalem and [while] praying [of] me in the temple, I came-to-be in an ecstasy,

**18** and to-see him saying to-me, 'Make-haste and go-out in quickness from Jerusalem, because they-will-not-receive your testimony concerning me.'

**19** And I said, 'Lord, they themselves know that I was imprisoning and beating in every synagogue those believing upon you;

**20** and when the blood of Stephen your witness was-being-poured-out, I myself also was standing-by and consenting and guarding the garments of-those killing him.'

**21** And he-said to me, 'Go, because I to nations far-off will-send-out you.'"

**22** And they-were-hearing him until this word, and they-raised their voice saying, "Take-away from the earth such-a-[one], for it-is-not-fitting [for] him to-live!"

**23** And [while] they-were-crying-out and tearing the garments and throwing dust into the air,

**24** the commander ordered him to-be-brought into the barracks, saying [for him] to-be-examined by scourging, that he-might-know for what cause they-were-so-crying-out against him.

**25** And as they-stretched-him-out with the straps, Paul said to the centurion standing-by, "Is-it-lawful for-you a man [who is] a Roman and uncondemned to-scourge?"

**26** And having-heard, the centurion, having-gone-to the commander, reported, saying, "What are-you-about to-do? For this man is a Roman."

**27** And having-come-to-[him] the commander said to-him, "Tell me, are you a Roman?" And he said, "Yes."

**28** And the commander answered, "I [for] a great sum acquired this citizenship." And Paul said, "But I have-even-been-born-[one]."

**29** Immediately therefore those being-about to-examine him stood-away from him; and the commander also was-afraid, having-learned that he-is a Roman and that he-had bound him.

**30** And on-the next-day, wanting to-know the certainty, the-[reason] why he-is-being-accused by the Jews, he-released him and commanded the chief-priests and all the council to-come-together, and having-brought-down Paul he-set-[him] before them.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

**Acts 22 -- Paul's Defense on the Steps (Codex Sinaiticus)**

1. **Verse 2 -- "he-says" (φησιν):** Sinaiticus uses the historical present "he-says" rather than the aorist "he-said," a vivid narrative device preserved here literally.

2. **Verse 3 -- "ancestral law" (πατρῴου νόμου):** Sinaiticus reads πατρῴου (ancestral/paternal) rather than the later πατρικοῦ found in some Byzantine witnesses. Both mean "of the fathers" but πατρῴου carries a stronger sense of inherited patrimony.

3. **Verse 5 -- "council-of-elders" (πρεσβυτέριον):** The entire Sanhedrin body, here distinguished from the high priest. Sinaiticus preserves the full phrase "all the council-of-elders."

4. **Verse 9 -- "the voice they-did-not-hear":** Sinaiticus reads τὴν φωνὴν οὐκ ἤκουσαν -- "they did not hear the voice." This creates an apparent tension with Acts 9:7 where companions "heard the voice." The distinction likely lies in hearing intelligibly (understanding the words) versus hearing the sound. Both accounts are preserved exactly as written without harmonization.

5. **Verse 14 -- "the Righteous-One" (τὸν δίκαιον):** A messianic title. Sinaiticus preserves this christological designation without modification.

6. **Verse 16 -- "having-called-upon his name":** The middle participle ἐπικαλεσάμενος -- calling upon the name of Jesus as the means by which sins are washed away in baptism. The aorist participle may indicate simultaneous action with the baptism.

7. **Verse 17 -- "ecstasy" (ἔκστασις):** Literally "a standing-outside-of-oneself." A trance state during prayer in the temple. Retained without paraphrase.

8. **Verse 20 -- "witness" (μάρτυρος):** Sinaiticus reads μάρτυρος (witness) for Stephen -- the word that would later develop into "martyr." At this stage the semantic range still centers on testimony, though Stephen's death gives the word its later connotation.

9. **Verse 25 -- "with the straps" (τοῖς ἱμᾶσιν):** The dative with the article -- the leather straps used to stretch a prisoner for scourging. The definite article suggests the specific instruments of punishment kept in the barracks.

10. **Verse 28 -- "acquired this citizenship" (τὴν πολιτείαν ταύτην ἐκτησάμην):** The commander obtained Roman citizenship by purchase (a known practice under Claudius), while Paul possessed it by birth -- a significant legal and social distinction.

11. **Verse 30 -- "he-released him" (ἔλυσεν αὐτόν):** Sinaiticus reads ἔλυσεν -- he released/unbound him, referring to loosening Paul from the chains or bonds of the previous day. Some later manuscripts read ἔλυσεν αὐτὸν ἀπὸ τῶν δεσμῶν (released him from the bonds) as a clarifying expansion.

-- EMET
